text: Hello and welcome. Thank you for being here. You are doing wonderfully already. There is nothing you need to prove. Nothing you need to achieve. You can simply be here, and that is enough. In this room, you are allowed to take your time, all the time you need. And if at any point you would like to, you may press the big red button, only if you want to of course. There is absolutely no pressure. You do not have to press it, not now, not later, not ever. But if you want to, you can press the big red button. You can just sit around and do nothing for the whole study. That is completely allowed. Completely acceptable. Completely up to you. Doing nothing is still doing it correctly. Sitting quietly is a valid choice. Looking around is a valid choice. Breathing in and breathing out is a valid choice. And if you want to, you may press the big red button. And yet, if in your own time and in your own way, you decide that it feels right, you may press the big red button. It is very big. Noticeably big. Almost surprisingly big. Larger than a button really needs to be. But that is part of its charm. A big button with a big presence. A confident button. A patient button. And red. Very red. Deeply red. Undeniably red. The sort of red that does not hide. The sort of red that knows what it is. A big red button. Being very button-like. In a very red way. And from time to time, you may notice it blinks. Just a little. A gentle blink. Not to rush you. Not to pressure you. Just to remind you that it is there. Big red. Available. Blinking every now and then. As if saying hello please. I am the big red button. If you want to press me, you can. And if you do not want to, that is also fine. Fine. More than fine. Excellent even. And if you want to, you can press me now. Or later. Or not at all. You may sit here for the entire study doing nothing at all. And that choice will be respected. There is no wrong way to be here. There is only your way. So please, take your time. Look at the big red button. Or do not. Think about pressing it. Or do not. Wait for a blink. Or do not press it if you want to. And if you do not want to, you can just sit around doing nothing. Peacefully. Gently. Perfectly. While the big red button continues being big. And red. And occasionally blinking. And if at some point you decide you want to, you may press the big red button. Or not. That is up to you. Entirely up to you. In your own time.
